seoTitle: New Lambton vs New Mexico — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between New Lambton and New Mexico. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: New Lambton vs New Mexico: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in New Lambton and New Mexico.
heading: New Lambton vs New Mexico — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between New Lambton, New South Wales and New Mexico, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: New Mexico has a lower overall climate risk score (38/100) compared to New Lambton (48/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is New Lambton or New Mexico safer from flooding?
faq.answer: New Lambton has a flood risk score of 55/100, while New Mexico scores 35/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: New Lambton scores 52/100 for bushfire risk. New Mexico scores 46/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in New Lambton or New Mexico?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. New Mexico has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.
